data.title: Triller faces additional Nasdaq delisting risk over missing SEC filings
data.description:
• Triller (ILLR) received a new Nasdaq delisting notice on Nov. 17, 2025, for failing to file its Q3 2025 results.
  • The company had already been issued a delisting notice on Oct. 14, 2025, for missing its 2024 Form 10-K and Q1–Q2 2025 Form 10-Qs.
  • Nasdaq indicated Triller’s stock could be suspended and delisted as early as Oct. 23, 2025, without a timely appeal.
  • Triller has requested a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel to contest the determination.

data.title: Kenvue gains amid speculation on potential rival bidder
data.description:
Kenvue (KVUE) rose 3% amid some speculation about a possible rival bidder after it agreed to be sold to Kimberly-Clark (KMB) for about $40 billion earlier this month. 

Another bidder may be evaluating making a counteroffer for Kenvue, possibly with a higher cash element or call cash, according to traders, who cited a Betavile "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Friday. The identity of the rival bidder is unclear.

There's some talk that Morgan Stanley was working for a company looking to buy Kenvue (KVUE) prior to the announcement of its deal with Kimberly-Clark (KMB), according to the report.  It's possible that Morgan Stanley may still be working with the rival suitor.

data.title: Inventiva S.A reports 9M results
data.description:
• Inventiva S.A (IVA): Revenues for the first nine months of 2025 amounted to €4.5 million, compared to none generated for the same period in 2024.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at €97.6 million, and €24.7 million in short-term deposits as of September 30, 2025.
  • Cash runway expected until the end of the first quarter of 2027.

data.title: Wall Street ended notably higher following the previous session’s violent swings
data.description:
Wall Street staged a rebound on Friday, closing the session in positive territory after a turbulent, tech-driven downturn rattled the Nasdaq Composite just a day earlier.

Friday's market boost was supported by reports that the Trump administration is considering allowing the sale of H200 GPUs to China.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed higher by 0.86%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) had ended up by 0.98% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) advanced by 1.08%.

On the week, the Nasdaq fell 2.74%, the S&P 500 dropped 1.95%, and the Dow came down 1.91%. 

"Following modest drops of 5-8% in the major equity indices, accompanied by rather more serious kickings administered to some high beta names and all of crypto, it’s possible the low is in for this correction,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research stated.

From a sector-by-sector point of view, all of the 11 S&P segments rounded out the session in the green and were led by Communication Services. At the other end of the spectrum, Utilities were the worst performer on the session.

Treasury yields ticked lower as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down by 2 basis points to 4.06% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down by 3 basis points to 3.51%. 

“While we expect volatility in 2026, we do expect a positive return with the S&P 500 reaching 7500 by the end of next year,” Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth said. 

“As we enter 2026, the equity market has had great performance since the lows of the bear market in 2022. We expect 2026 to be the reset year for the leadership of the market in the big Growth Technology stocks. We believe this reset will position stocks to continue their leadership into the end of the decade. Despite this anticipated correction in the large cap area of the market, we don’t see leadership emerging from small caps,” Mary Ann Bartels added. 

On the economic front, there were a few Federal Reserve speakers that addressed the market, including Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan and Boston Fed President Susan Collins. 

The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose slightly to 54.8 in November from 54.6 in the prior month. At the same time, Services PMI came in at 55 and manufacturing PMI came in at 51.9.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Intuit Inc (INTU) closed higher by 3.8%, while shares of Oracle (ORCL) slid lower by 5.7% on Friday.

data.title: Ucore Rare Metals started Buy at B Riley, with RapidSX poised to scale meaningfully
data.description:
Ucore Rare Metals (OTCQX:UURAF) +3.4% in Friday's trading as B Riley initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $9 price target, seeing the company as a pure-play investment opportunity in the midstream separation step of the rare earth supply chain, thanks to its proprietary RapidSX Technology, which is poised to scale meaningfully in 2026 at the Louisiana Strategic Metals Complex.

B Riley's Nick Giles said he sees multiple potential catalysts ahead for Ucore (OTCQX:UURAF) that could drive shares higher, including development of the Louisiana Strategic Metals Complex for commercial scale TREO production in 2026 and positive EBITDA in 2027; the translation of multiple upstream and downstream MoUs into definitive agreements; the scale-up of a first-of-a-kind processing facility dedicated to samarium and gadolinium; and any escalation in China's curbs on rare earths or increased incentives for U.S. producers, such as price floors.

The analyst pointed to Ucore's (OTCQX:UURAF) patented and demonstrated RapidSX technology as a "critical differentiator" from conventional solvent extraction, citing its compact design, which reduces footprint, efficiently blends solutions 3x faster than conventional methods, and adapts to diverse feedstocks, making it a one-stop solution with much lower processing costs than traditional methods.

data.title: Frontline signals $1.8B cash generation potential while reinforcing VLCC market focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Frontline plc (FRO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lars Barstad described the current tanker market as highly energetic, stating, "We have previously argued that this market owes us money, and we have finally started to collect some of it." Barstad highlighted that Frontline achieved $34,300 per day on its VLCC fleet, $35,100 per day on the Suezmax fleet, and $31,400 per day on the LR2/Aframax fleet for the third quarter. He noted, "So far in the third quarter of '25, we have booked 75% of our VLCC days at $83,300 per day, 75% of our Suezmax days at $60,600 per day and 51% of our LR2/Aframax days at $42,200 per day."
  • CFO Inger Klemp stated, "We report profit of $40.3 million or $0.18 per share and adjusted profit of $42.5 million or $0.19 per share in the third quarter." Klemp added, "Frontline has a solid balance sheet and strong liquidity of $819 million in cash and cash equivalents, including undrawn amounts of revolver capacity, marketable securities and minimum cash requirements bank as of September 30, 2025."
  • Klemp further outlined, "We converted 7 existing credit facilities with aggregate outstanding term loan balances of $405.5 million and undrawn revolving credit capacity of $87.8 million into revolving reducing credit facilities of up to $493.4 million in September 2025." She also noted a prepayment of $374.2 million, leading to a reduction in fleet average cash breakeven rate.

Outlook

  • Barstad pointed to potential for a "sustained contango structure in the oil market developing," and described the VLCC-centric trade pattern as returning, driven by increased Atlantic Basin exports to Asia. Management indicated that if current supply trends continue, a "prolonged period of oversupply" in global oil supply and demand may occur, with possible interesting trading developments for shipping.
  • Klemp estimated average cash breakeven rates for the next 12 months of approximately $26,000 per day for VLCCs, $23,300 per day for Suezmax tankers, and $23,600 per day for LR2 tankers, with a fleet average estimate of about $24,700 per day including dry dock costs. Klemp added, "the cash generation potential basis current fleet and TCE rates...is $1.8 billion or $8.15 per share, providing a cash flow yield of 33% basis current share price."

Financial Results

  • Adjusted profit in the third quarter decreased by $37.8 million compared with the previous quarter, primarily due to a decrease in time charter earnings from $283 million to $248 million, a result of lower TCE rates and fluctuations in other income and expenses. Ship operating expenses increased by $3.1 million, with a noted decrease in supplier rebates and costs related to ship management changes.
  • The administrative expenses, excluding synthetic option revaluation, decreased by $0.2 million from the previous quarter.
  • OpEx, including dry dock in the third quarter, was $9,000 per day for VLCCs, $8,100 per day for Suezmax tankers, and $9,100 per day for LR2 tankers. The Q3 '25 average OpEx, excluding dry dock, was $8,500 per day.

Q&A

  • Jonathan Chappell, Evercore ISI: Asked about the company's approach to deleveraging versus maintaining strong dividends. Barstad responded, "We are different from our peers. We're actually not particularly comfortable working with this kind of fairly low LTVs...We want our assets to generate cash as quickly as possible."
  • Chappell followed up on scrapping assumptions. Barstad explained that older vessels often shift to sanctioned trades, stating, "The alternative use that's been going on ever since 2019 or '18, '19 is the trade of sanctioned oil...We circle around the compliant market, and we relate ourselves to the compliant oil market."
  • Sherif Elmaghrabi, BTIG: Inquired about the dark fleet and contango's effect on demand. Barstad commented, "There are an increasing amount of vessels just sitting at anchor with no crew on...But the incremental barrel now does not come from the sanction nations. It actually comes from the compliant fleet, and that's the only part of the market we really care about."
  • Omar Nokta, Jefferies: Asked about LR2 fleet strategy and potential sales. Barstad declined to comment directly on sales but indicated a strategic focus on expanding the VLCC fleet, stating, "Long term, if we were to divest of the LR2s...I think it would be natural for us to focus on the big guns on the VLCCs."
  • Unknown Analyst: Queried about floating storage and sanctions. Barstad clarified, "The storage that we are seeing right now is more due to logistics or distress or weather. So it's not commercial in that way."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on the sustainability of high rates, deleveraging, and market structure. There was pressing on asset sales and fleet utilization, but no overt skepticism.
  • Management's prepared remarks reflected high confidence and optimism: "We are positively excited by the developments in this market." During Q&A, Barstad was direct but cautious, especially regarding asset sales and future market conditions, showing restraint in making categorical predictions.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone shifted from concern about market volatility to a more constructive focus on capital allocation and strategy. Management's tone evolved from cautious optimism to pronounced confidence.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious (Q2: "admittedly somewhat short of expectations") to highly optimistic in Q3, with strong emphasis on cash generation potential and favorable market dynamics.
  • Strategic focus moved more explicitly to VLCC expansion and away from LR2s, as inferred from repeated emphasis on the VLCC trade pattern and economies of scale.
  • Key metrics declined quarter-over-quarter: Adjusted profit and time charter earnings both fell, and cash breakeven rates were reduced through debt prepayments.
  • Management’s confidence increased, with more assertive language on market positioning and shareholder returns. Analysts' tone became more inquisitive about capital structure and market longevity, departing from previous focus on short-term volatility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted the logistical challenges around sanctioned oil trade and highlighted the risk of an expanding sanctioned/dark fleet. Barstad noted, "There is a discussion ongoing to -- if one can kind of set up some sort of mechanism where against a fine, you can actually access the recycling market, but only the recycling market alone."
  • Industry risks include fluctuating oil prices, sustained high freight rates, and the potential for regulatory tightening on sanctioned vessels.
  • Ship operating expenses rose, and management acknowledged cost pressures from supplier rebates and ship management transitions.

Final Takeaway

Frontline sees a resurgence in the VLCC market with robust cash generation potential, supported by strong spot rates and positive trade patterns. Management reiterated its commitment to shareholder returns and strategic fleet positioning, while highlighting ongoing market uncertainties and operational cost challenges. The company’s reinforced focus on VLCC expansion and disciplined capital structure signals continued confidence in the tanker market’s outlook.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tech Voices: Trump shelves AI order, China chip sales, Nvidia-Foxconn
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of remarks, statements, and announcements that could impact the technology sector.

  • The Trump administration is temporarily shelving a draft executive order that aimed to preempt state laws regulating AI with less restrictive federal guidelines.

The EO sought to block state laws through legal challenges and withdrawal of federal funds, Reuters reported on Friday, citing people close to the matter.

Reuters said the EO directed Attorney General Pam Bondi to establish an AI Litigation Task Force "whose sole responsibility shall be to challenge state AI laws, including on grounds that such laws unconstitutionally regulate interstate commerce, are preempted by existing federal regulations, or are otherwise unlawful."

  • U.S. officials have been debating whether to allow Nvidia (NVDA) to sell its H200 AI chips to China, according to Bloomberg.

The news outlet added that the discussions were early-stage and may not result in Nvidia receiving licensing approval for the sales under current U.S. export restrictions.

  • Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) said that a $1.4 billion data center it's building with Nvidia (NVDA) in Taiwan will be operational during the first half of 2026.

The 27-megawatt data center, which will be powered by Nvidia's new Blackwell GB300 chips, will be Asia's first GB300 data center, FoxConn said during a tech day event on Friday, according to Reuters. 

"As GPU technology accelerates, building individual facilities may no longer make economic sense," said Nvidia senior executive Alexis Bjorlin at the event, which was also attended by executives from clients and partners such as OpenAI (OPENAI) and Uber (UBER). 

"Renting compute resources may offer a far better return on investment, enabling flexibility and enabling companies to scale their compute according to both product and business cycles," Bjorlin added.

data.title: DNOW upgraded at Susquehanna as MRC acquisition opens new chapter
data.description:
DNOW (DNOW) +8.3% in Friday's trading as Susquehanna upgraded shares to Positive from Neutral with a $16 price target, saying the company is a more formidable energy and industrial solutions provider with the MRC Global acquisition now closed, with a broader, more diversified suite of product offerings in its core end markets.

The acquisition also provides increased scale, greater opportunities for growth in energy evolution and industrial markets, a clean balance sheet allowing for further M&A, and solid free cash flow for debt reduction and shareholder returns, according to Susquehanna's Charles Minervino, adding that DNOW's (DNOW) seasoned management team with a long history of experience in the sector will help unlock profitable growth in the coming years.

The combined company, which will operate more than 350 service and distribution locations in more than 20 countries, is expected to generate $70M of annual cost synergies within three years after the deal closes.

At ~6x FY 2026 pro forma EBITDA, the analyst sees an attractive entry point for DNOW (DNOW).

data.title: Elanco's Credelio CAT gets FDA Emergency Use Authorization
data.description:
• Elanco Animal Health (ELAN) has received Emergency Use Authorization from the U.S. FDA for its product, Credelio CAT, which is designed to treat New World screwworm infestations in cats.
  • This authorization is based on research that assessed the effectiveness of Credelio CAT against Old World screwworms in cats and the performance of Credelio in treating New World screwworms in dogs.

data.title: Google continues to test ads in AI Mode results (update)
data.description:
• Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) is still testing ads in AI Mode results, a PR source on behalf of the company clarified on Friday.
  • In a post on X, Brodie Clark, an independent SEO consultant, claimed the company started rolling out ads in AI mode for the first time ever.  But according to the PR firm representing Google, "People seeing ads in AI Mode in the wild is simply part of Google’s ongoing tests, which we’ve been running for several months."
  • "We first announced this testing at Google Marketing Live, and we do not have any new updates to share related to roll-out," according to the PR rep.
  • The company began testing ads in AI Mode in May this year after a positive response from showing ads in AI Overviews.
